bids.reasoning: At this early stage in the negotiation, there is no time pressure to accept a low-utility offer. The opponent's initial offer has the lowest possible utility for me given all issues' values have the least preferred utility scores. I will counter with a high-utility offer that reflects my preferences, sticking to my top preferred values for each issue to secure the highest possible utility.

At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.02), my priority is to maximize my utility by sticking closely to my preferred values. The opponent's offers so far have been significantly lower in utility for me, indicating they are setting an anchor or testing the waters. There's no evidence yet of their willingness to concede or which issues they value more. Thus, I'll propose an offer close to my ideal to maintain a strong starting position.
